name: CWL On Slurm
summary: Run small CWL workflows inside Slurm allocations with cwltool.
description: This skill provides a conservative pattern for running Common Workflow Language workflows with cwltool inside a Slurm allocation, including output, cache, and temporary directory controls, a tiny CommandLineTool smoke test, and dry-run-first launch records.

inputs.name: cwl_document
inputs.description: CWL Workflow, CommandLineTool, or ExpressionTool document.

inputs.name: job_file
inputs.description: YAML or JSON input object for the CWL run.

inputs.name: work_dirs
inputs.description: Cache and temporary directories appropriate for the Slurm allocation and filesystem.

outputs.name: cwl_launch_plan
outputs.description: Reviewable cwltool command, paths, cache, temporary directories, and Slurm allocation details.

outputs.name: cwl_outputs
outputs.description: CWL output files written to the configured output directory when RUN_CWL=1.

outputs.name: run_record
outputs.description: Log file with cwltool version, validation status, and command provenance.

examples.title: Plan or run a CWL smoke test in Slurm

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.
Run the wrapper in default dry-run mode, then set RUN_CWL=1 on a short allocation after loading a site-approved cwltool environment.
